title: CRM Selection Guide for Growing Businesses
description: Find the right CRM for your sales team with our structured selection process.

sections.title: Assess Your Sales Process
sections.body: Map out your current sales pipeline stage by stage, from lead generation to close. Identify bottlenecks, manual steps, and data you are not capturing but should be. A CRM should mirror and enhance your process — not force you into an unfamiliar workflow.

sections.title: Must-Have CRM Features
sections.body: Core features to evaluate include contact management, deal tracking, pipeline visualization, and reporting. Look for automation capabilities like email sequencing, task assignment, and follow-up reminders. Advanced features such as lead scoring and AI insights can give your team a competitive edge.

sections.title: Consider Integration Needs
sections.body: Your CRM must sync with your email provider, calendar, marketing platform, and any accounting software you use. Check native integrations before considering third-party middleware like Zapier. Tight integration prevents data silos and keeps your sales team working from a single source of truth.

sections.title: Evaluate Usability
sections.body: A powerful CRM is useless if your team refuses to use it. Request trial access and have your sales reps click through daily tasks like logging calls and updating deals. Prioritize tools with intuitive interfaces, mobile apps, and minimal data entry friction.

sections.title: Plan for Data Migration
sections.body: Export your existing contacts, deals, and activity history and map them to the new CRM's data model. Clean duplicates and standardize formats before importing to avoid polluting your new system. Run a full test migration before the final cutover to catch issues early.

author: StackPilot Team
